what_it_does: Declares a projection registry row named 'operators' that describes registered OPERATOR roles as an embedded vector space, so content no operator covers accumulates as a candidate new operator.
observations: The file contains only a module docstring and a single PROJECTION dictionary.
PROJECTION['id'] is 'operators' and the docstring states it must equal the file stem.
'embeds' is True and 'field' is 'text', indicating this projection is embedded over text.
The docstring references runtime/projection.py and projection/AGENTS.md.
The description uses specialized terms: 'vector SPACE', 'nucleation types_space', 'GATE/promotion keystone', 'seed §11'.
standouts: The projection id is required to match the file stem ('operators').
It frames operator roles as a vector space whose gaps indicate candidate new operators.
It is explicitly marked as an embedded ('embeds': True) structural projection produced by code.
conventions: Follows a declarative-direct projection pattern: a module-level PROJECTION dict with metadata keys.
Uses a docstring to identify the file, its id constraint, and related documentation.
Keeps projection metadata self-contained in one dictionary.
concerns: No executable code or validation enforces that id equals the file stem.
The description is dense with project-specific jargon and may be hard to parse without external context.
No symbols are defined, so the file contributes only metadata.
notes: A metadata-only projection declaration that treats operator roles as an embedded vector space for discovering uncovered content.
questions: How is the operator vector space actually constructed and updated?
What logic implements the 'GATE/promotion keystone' for promoting uncovered content to a new operator?
Where is the list of registered operators maintained and how does runtime/projection.py consume this row?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to register a projection describing operator roles; the actual content is a metadata dictionary that does exactly that, so it matches.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make operator roles queryable as an embedded semantic space so that un-covered content can be detected and promoted into a new operator role.
novelty: The framing of operator roles as a vector space and of uncovered content 'piling up' into a candidate new operator via a promotion gate is unusual.
contribution: Provides a named, self-describing projection row that documents the operators role-space and its intended use for coverage-gap nucleation, which a fused system can reference when reasoning about operator discovery.
summary_for_embedding: projection/operators.py declares an embedded structural projection named 'operators' that describes registered operator roles as a text vector space for identifying uncovered content as candidate new operators.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to register the operators projection so the runtime can embed operator roles and detect gaps that may nucleate new operators.
